situational_context: Running comeback week 4: 5 runs completed since May 3 (after 182-day gap). Run #5 (May 23): 5.02 km, HR avg 136, max 153, suffer 19 — best execution yet at 5 km, showing clear cardiac efficiency gains vs Run #4 (HR 140, suffer 26). Primary modality remains eBike commuting (~38 km round trip on sur-site days). Chronic load declining (17.9, down from 22.3 on May 14) as early-May higher loads exit the 28-day window — not alarming given stable acute load and optimal ACWR (0.95). Training readiness excellent (87, HIGH). HRV stable at 37 (BALANCED). RHR 48 bpm — lowest reading in weeks. Weight stable ~103.8 kg (-0.3 vs May 14). Paris heatwave this week (33-34C Wed-Fri, cooling to 24C Sunday). Conges Thu-Fri allow ideal session placement. Nutritionist RDV May 28 (Lucie UGUEN). Comeback protocol active — consolidating at 5 km before progression.

recommendations.text: Execute Run #6 tomorrow (May 28) at 12:00: 5 km Z1-Z2, HR target <=138 bpm. Canicule precautions mandatory (34C) — hydrate 500 ml pre-run, carry water, shaded paths (Bois de Vincennes), reduce pace if HR >145 (heat drift). Stop if dizziness/nausea. Today's eBike commute was light (suffer 14) — no adjustment needed.
recommendations.measurable: Run #6 completed at <=5 km, avg HR <=140, zero localized pain at 24h

recommendations.text: Discuss protein distribution with nutritionist Lucie UGUEN (May 28, 10:15): trailing 7d average is 135g/day vs 150g target. Most deficit at breakfast. Discuss 0.4g/kg/repas threshold for MPS optimization at 104 kg body weight.
recommendations.measurable: 7d protein avg >=140g by next week; actionable plan from nutritionist

recommendations.text: Execute both weekend sessions (Sat velo Z2 50 min + Sun Run #7 5 km). If Saturday storms force cancellation, shift velo to Sun AM and run to Sun PM with >=3h gap. Continue daily Vitamin D supplementation.
recommendations.measurable: Both weekend sessions executed; Vitamin D supplementation confirmed daily

markers_to_watch.threshold: Watch for stabilization above 17; if continues below 16, reassess weekly volume
markers_to_watch.reason: Chronic load declined from 22.3 to 17.9 over 13 days as early-May loads roll out — expected decay, but continued decline signals insufficient stimulus

markers_to_watch.threshold: Alert if RHR rises >5 bpm above current 49 bpm for 3+ consecutive days
markers_to_watch.reason: RHR at 48 bpm — excellent. Elevation would indicate overload or illness during heatwave

markers_to_watch.threshold: Any localized joint/tendon pain lasting >24h after Run #6 triggers skip of Run #7
markers_to_watch.reason: Tissue tolerance bottleneck at 103.8 kg; heatwave adds thermal stress on top of mechanical load

markers_to_watch.threshold: If HR during Run #6 is >10 bpm above Run #5 at similar pace, attribute to heat drift not fitness loss
markers_to_watch.reason: 34C expected Thursday; cardiac drift of 5-10 bpm is normal in heat
